6: /// Represents a type of event that callbacks can be registered for.
7: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
8: pub struct EventType(pub String);
9: 
10: impl EventType {
11:     pub fn new(name: &str) -> Self {
12:         EventType(name.to_string())
13:     }
14: 
15:     pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
16:         &self.0
17:     }
18: }
19: 
20: /// Defines the action to be taken upon a callback error.
21: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
22: pub enum CallbackAction {
23:     /// Continue processing, but return the error to the caller.
24:     Continue,
25:     /// Attempt to roll back the action that triggered the callback.
26:     Rollback,
27: }
28: 
29: /// Represents an error returned by a callback.
30: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
31: pub struct CallbackError {
32:     pub message: String,
33:     pub action: CallbackAction,
34: }
35: 
36: /// The result type for a callback function.
37: /// On success, it can optionally return a message (`Ok(Some(String))`).
38: /// On failure, it returns a `CallbackError`.
39: pub type CallbackResult = Result<Option<String>, CallbackError>;
40: 
41: /// Type alias for a callback function.
42: /// It receives a mutable context object and additional parameters.
43: pub type CallbackFn = Box<dyn FnMut(&mut dyn Any, &[&dyn Any]) -> CallbackResult + Send + Sync + 'static>;
44: 
45: /// Registry for managing callback functions.
46: pub struct CallbackManager {
47:     callbacks: HashMap<EventType, Vec<CallbackFn>>,
48: }
49: 
50: lazy_static! {
51:     /// The global callback manager.
52:     pub static ref CALLBACK_MANAGER: Mutex<CallbackManager> = Mutex::new(CallbackManager::new());
53: }
54: 
55: impl CallbackManager {
56:     /// Creates a new empty callback manager.
57:     fn new() -> Self {
58:         Self {
59:             callbacks: HashMap::new(),
60:         }
61:     }
62: 
63:     /// Registers a callback function for a given event type.
64:     pub fn register_callback(&mut self, event_type: EventType, callback: CallbackFn) {
65:         self.callbacks
66:             .entry(event_type)
67:             .or_default()
68:             .push(callback);
69:     }
70: 
71:     /// Triggers all registered callback functions for a given event type.
72:     ///
73:     /// # Arguments
74:     /// * `event_type` - The event to trigger callbacks for.
75:     /// * `context` - A mutable reference to a context object (e.g., `GenericDataObject`) that the callback can operate on.
76:     /// * `params` - A slice of additional parameters for the callback.
77:     ///
78:     /// # Returns
79:     /// * `Ok(Vec<String>)` - A vector of success messages from all callbacks that returned one.
80:     /// * `Err(CallbackError)` - The error from the first callback that failed.
81:     pub fn trigger_callbacks(
82:         &mut self,
83:         event_type: &EventType,
84:         context: &mut dyn Any,
85:         params: &[&dyn Any],
86:     ) -> Result<Vec<String>, CallbackError> {
87:         let mut messages = Vec::new();
88: 
89:         if let Some(callbacks) = self.callbacks.get_mut(event_type) {
90:             for callback in callbacks {
91:                 match callback(context, params) {
92:                     Ok(Some(message)) => messages.push(message),
93:                     Ok(None) => { /* Continue processing */ }
94:                     Err(e) => return Err(e), // Stop on first error
95:                 }
96:             }
97:         }
98: 
99:         Ok(messages)
100:     }
